echo ==================================================
echo  Upload to %DEST% - Start    %date% %time%
echo ==================================================

:: --------------------------------------------------
:: Backup existing folders to E:\micronet\backup\
:: --------------------------------------------------
set BACKUP=%DEST%\backup
set TIMESTAMP=%date:~0,4%%date:~5,2%%date:~8,2%_%time:~0,2%%time:~3,2%%time:~6,2%
set TIMESTAMP=%TIMESTAMP: =0%
set BACKUP_DIR=%BACKUP%\%TIMESTAMP%

echo [Backup] Saving current files to %BACKUP_DIR% ...
if not exist "%BACKUP_DIR%" mkdir "%BACKUP_DIR%"

The user is asking if the backup is accidentally backing up files that aren't part of the upload set. Let me check the current script to verify.

The backup backs up: bin, dll, exe, file, tool, Web, E_mail

Well, E_mail might have files in the destination that were put there by other means. Backing it up before the upload makes sense since we're not touching its contents anyway... actually, we're NOT touching E_mail's contents during upload, we're only creating the directory. 

Since the upload never modifies anything inside E_mail, there's no point backing it up beforehand. I should remove E_mail from the backup list.
